title: let-else in Session capability methods is the correct Rust idiom for bind-or-bail
description: Session::chat_capability, chat_push_capability, and websocket_capability each need to look up a value in a HashMap and return Ok(None) early if absent. The prior match-with-early-return required a full match arm at the call site and nested the happy-path logic inside the Some arm. let-else (Rust 1.65+) is the canonical form for this pattern: it keeps the bail-out condition co-located with the binding, leaves the happy path at the left margin without extra indentation, and makes the control flow direction obvious at a glance. A future contributor should not revert to match or if-let chains to support older MSRV — the project already uses Rust 1.65+ features elsewhere. The let-else form is not just style; it reduces the risk of accidentally falling through into the happy path when adding new early-return conditions.
